---
title: "Case Study on Portland, OR"
---

In this section you can see the culmination of all that I have learned in this class. I perform a case study on demographic change in my hometown, Portland, OR. Specifically I look at the entire CBSA, which spans two states (Oregon and Washington) and has a population of approximately 2.5 million people.

![Here is a view of the census tracts in the Portland-Vancouver-Hillsboro CBSA](pdx_within_cbsa.png){fig-align="center" width="535"}

There are 568 census tracts in the CBSA.

```{oregon_sex <- get_estimates(}
  geography = "state",
  state = "OR",
  product = "characteristics",
  breakdown = c("SEX", "AGEGROUP"),
  breakdown_labels = TRUE,
  year = 2019
)

oregon_filtered <- filter(oregon_sex, str_detect(AGEGROUP, "^Age"),
                        SEX != "Both sexes") %>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(SEX == "Male",
                        -value, value))
oregon_pyramid_1 <- ggplot(oregon_filtered, aes(x = value, y = AGEGROUP, fill = SEX)) +
  geom_col()

oregon_pyramid_1

oregon_pyramid_2 <- ggplot(oregon_filtered,
                       aes(x = value,
                           y = AGEGROUP,
                           fill = SEX)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.95, alpha = 0.75) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana",
                base_size = 12) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~ number_format(scale = .001, suffix = "k")(abs(.x)),
    limits = 160000 * c(-1,1)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = ~ str_remove_all(.x, "Age\\s|\\syears")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("deeppink", "deepskyblue")) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "2019 Census Bureau population estimate",
       title = "Population structure in Oregon",
       fill = "",
       caption = "Sources: US Census Bureau PEP, tidycensus R package")
oregon_pyramid_2
```

```{or <- get_acs(}
  geography = "tract",
  variables = "B19013_001",
  state = "OR",
  county = "Multnomah",
  geometry = TRUE,
  year = 2020,
  cb = FALSE
) %>%
  st_transform(8316)

#or_crs <- suggest_crs(or)
#or_crs

or_erase <- erase_water(or, area_threshold = 0.8)

or_water_erase <- ggplot(or_erase) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = estimate)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::label_dollar()) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(fill = "Median household\nincome",
       title = "Median Household Income by Census tract, Multnomah County",
       caption = "Data source: 2016-2020 ACS via the tidycensus R package.")

or_water_erase
```

```{}
```
